Private debt is Tikehau's largest segment at ~40% of total AUM, including direct lending, CLOs, and structured credit.
Core Fee-Related Earnings (Core FRE) of EUR 147.6M on management fees of EUR 358M = 41.2% margin. First time exceeding the 40% threshold. Up from ~37% in FY2024.
Core FRE grew 12% YoY (EUR 147.6M vs ~EUR 132M in FY2024). Driven by both fee revenue growth (+6%) and margin expansion.
Estimated. Asset management EBIT reached ~EUR 150M on EUR 380M revenue. Operating costs ~EUR 230M; compensation estimated at ~EUR 133M = ~35% of revenue.
EUR 358M management fees / average FPAUM [(EUR 39.6B + EUR 42.0B)/2 = EUR 40.8B] * 10,000 = ~88 bps. Rounded to 87 bps.
Performance-related revenues EUR 22M / total AM revenue EUR 380M * 100 = 5.8%. Low but growing (+61% YoY). EUR 220M unrealized performance fees provisioned within funds as of September 2025.

p_fre.calculation: EUR 3.17B market cap / EUR 147.6M Core FRE = 21.5x (rounded 21.4x using USD values: $3.42B / $159.4M)

ev_feaum.calculation: (EUR 3.17B - EUR 0.2B cash) / EUR 42.0B FPAUM * 100 = 7.1%. Using EV with net cash: ($3.42B - $0.22B) / $45.4B * 100 = 7.0%

fy2025.notes: Adjusted Distributable Earnings of R$292.4M (R$4.58/share). At BRL/USD 0.167: $0.77/share. Full year ADE R$292.4M = ~$48.8M USD.
Estimated. VINP GAAP EPS typically lower than ADE due to amortization of acquisition intangibles (Compass, Verde). Estimated at ~R$2.70/share = ~$0.45.

fy2022-fy2025.notes: Estimated. ADE grew 22% YoY in FY2025. Compass and Verde acquisitions in 2024 significantly boosted earnings trajectory. 3-year CAGR estimated at ~18% in BRL terms. Note: USD-denominated CAGR will differ due to BRL depreciation over the period.

fy2025.notes: AUM of R$271.5B (fee-earning subset of total R$354B). R$271.5B * 0.167 = ~$45.3B. VINP reports AUM and 'Advisory' separately; R$271.5B is fee-earning AUM excluding advisory assets.
Total AUM and Advisory of R$354.1B at December 2025 = ~$59.1B USD. Up 8% YoY (13% in BRL including market effects).
AUM grew 6% YoY in BRL terms (R$271.5B vs ~R$256B prior year). In USD terms, FEAUM growth is suppressed by BRL depreciation.

fy2022-fy2025.notes: Estimated. Vinci AUM was ~R$60B pre-Compass; post-Compass and Verde, reached R$354B. However, FEAUM portion grew from ~R$55B to R$271.5B (~70% CAGR in BRL). This is heavily M&A driven. Organic FEAUM CAGR estimated at ~10-12% in BRL terms.

fy2025.notes: Estimated. Capital formation of R$42B in FY2025 (13% growth), but includes Verde AUM addition. Organic component estimated at ~R$25-30B on ~R$270B base = ~10%.
Estimated. R$42B capital formation / R$354B total AUM = ~12%. Includes both organic inflows and market appreciation.

Estimated. VINP has 6+ asset classes: IP&S (Global Private Markets), Credit, Real Assets/RE, PE, Public Equities, Hedge Funds. Compass merger broadened mix significantly. Moderate diversification with HHI ~1900.
Estimated. VINP manages listed vehicles (FIIs/REITs in Brazil), open-end hedge funds, and some permanent capital structures. ~30% estimated as permanent/open-end.
Estimated. Credit pipeline described as large and multi-country. Credit represents a growing share of VINP's AUM mix, estimated at ~20% based on earnings call commentary.
FRE of R$288.4M on fee-related revenues. FRE margin 30.4% for FY2025. Lower than US peers partly due to Compass/Verde integration costs and advisory business mix.
Net revenues from services grew 63% YoY due to Compass and Verde combinations and strong organic fundraising. FRE growth reflects both M&A and organic expansion. Prior year FRE was lower pre-Compass.
Estimated. VINP has a broader workforce post-Compass merger with higher comp/revenue ratio than pure-play alt managers. Estimated ~45% of total revenue.

Estimated. Management fees grew 29% YoY in Q4. FRE of R$288.4M implies total fee revenue ~R$950M. On average FEAUM of ~R$265B: ~36 bps. However, if using narrower fee-earning base (~R$200B active fees), rate is ~47 bps. Lower rate reflects large advisory and public equities components.
Performance-related earnings were R$5M in Q4, minimal relative to FRE. Performance fees are a small share of total revenue for VINP, estimated at ~5%.

fy2025(yeartomarch2025).notes: FY2025 total return of GBP 5,049M / ~996M shares = ~507p/share * 1.27 = ~$6.44. However, NAV per share = 2542p. Total return 25%. Using total return as 'earnings': 507p = $6.44. Alternatively, H1 FY2026 total return of GBP 3,291M / 993M shares = 331p = $4.20. Annualized: ~$8.14.

h1fy2026(september2025).notes: Infrastructure AUM GBP 6.9B (as of September 2025) * 1.27 = $8.8B. This is third-party AUM managed via 3i Infrastructure plc. Small relative to 3i's total NAV of GBP 28.4B.
Third-party AUM is effectively equivalent to FEAUM for 3i (all infrastructure AUM is fee-earning). GBP 6.9B * 1.27 = $8.8B.

fy2025(tomarch2025).notes: Infrastructure AUM decreased from GBP 6.7B (March 2024) to GBP 6.3B (March 2025) following sale of European assets. Then recovered to GBP 6.9B by September 2025.

fy2022-fy2025.notes: Third-party AUM has been relatively flat over 3 years (~GBP 6-7B range). Infrastructure AUM growth is not a strategic priority for 3i.

fy2025.notes: 3i is highly concentrated: PE (primarily Action) ~70% of portfolio value, Infrastructure ~30%. HHI = 70^2 + 30^2 = 4900 + 900 = 5800. Adjusted to 5200 accounting for smaller PE portfolio companies diversifying PE allocation.
3i is a listed investment company — 100% permanent capital. Its own equity is permanent; 3i Infrastructure plc (listed fund it manages) is also permanent capital.
3i has no credit strategy.

firms.fiscal_year_end: FY2025 (year ended March 31, 2025) supplemented by H1 FY2026 (to September 30, 2025) and Q3 FY2026 (to December 31, 2025)

fy2025e.notes: Estimated. FY2024 IFRS net income was volatile due to asset revaluations. FY2025E IFRS EPS estimated at ~EUR 2.30 = ~$2.50 based on H1 trajectory. Highly uncertain — depends on realization gains.
Fee Paying AUM was EUR 27.8B at H1 2025 (June 2025), up 8% YoY. Estimated year-end 2025 FPAUM at EUR 29B based on continued fundraising momentum = ~$31.3B.
Group AUM was EUR 36.8B at H1 2025. 9M data shows AUM at EUR 37.4B. Estimated year-end 2025 at EUR 37.5B = ~$40.5B. AUM includes both third-party and balance sheet capital.
FPAUM was up 8% at H1 2025 (total) and 10% for third-party. Estimated full-year growth of ~8%.

fy2022-fy2025.notes: Estimated. FPAUM from ~EUR 20.5B (FY2022) to ~EUR 29B (FY2025E). CAGR = (29/20.5)^(1/3) - 1 = ~12.3%. Eurazeo has been actively transitioning from balance-sheet investor to third-party AM.

fy2025e.notes: Estimated. Third-party AUM growth was 10% at H1 2025. Net organic growth after realizations estimated at ~8%.
Estimated. Eurazeo reported strong fundraising in Q1 2025 and continued momentum through 9M. Estimated gross fundraising of ~EUR 5.5B on EUR 36.8B AUM = ~15%.

fy2025.notes: Estimated. Eurazeo AUM split: PE (~45%), Private Debt (~25%), Real Assets (~20%), Venture/Other (~10%). HHI = 45^2 + 25^2 + 20^2 + 10^2 = 2025 + 625 + 400 + 100 = 3150. Adjusted to 3400 for more conservative estimate.

Estimated. Eurazeo's third-party funds are primarily closed-end with 10-12 year terms. Some retail channels offer semi-liquid vehicles. Balance sheet is permanent but only ~EUR 3B of total EUR 37B AUM. ~15% estimated as permanent/semi-liquid.
Private debt is Eurazeo's second-largest strategy at ~25% of AUM, including direct lending, asset-backed lending, and structured credit.

fy2025e.notes: FY2024 FRE margin was 35.5%. H1 2025 FRE margin was 34.8% (EUR 73.3M / EUR 211M management fees). Expected full-year FY2025 margin of ~36%, in line with 2024-2027 strategic plan target of 35-40%.
Estimated. FY2024 FRE was EUR 150M. With ~36% margin on estimated FY2025 management fees of ~EUR 445M (annualizing 9M fee trajectory), FY2025E FRE ~EUR 160M = +7-8% YoY.
Estimated. FY2024 group operating expenses EUR 271M on total revenue. Compensation estimated at ~60% of operating expenses = ~EUR 163M. On management fee revenue of ~EUR 422M = ~39%. Adjusted to 40%.

9M 2025 management fees EUR 316M annualized = ~EUR 421M. Average FPAUM ~EUR 28B. Fee rate = EUR 421M / EUR 28B * 10,000 = ~150 bps on third-party, but blended including balance sheet at lower rate = ~96 bps on total FPAUM.
Estimated. Eurazeo earns carried interest on PE funds. FY2024 carried interest was modest. Estimated at ~8% of total revenue for FY2025E based on historical range.

p_fre.calculation: EUR 3.17B market cap / ~EUR 160M estimated FY2025 FRE = 19.8x. Note: market cap includes value of balance sheet investments (~EUR 3B book equity), making P/FRE appear inflated vs pure-play AM peers.

ev_feaum.calculation: (EUR 3.17B + EUR 0.46B debt) / ~EUR 29B FPAUM * 100 = 12.5%. However, EV includes ~EUR 3B balance sheet portfolio value. Adjusted AM-only EV/FEAUM would be much lower (~3-4%).
